messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show resources command do?

The show resources command shows hardware resource consumption on the switch. Resource data is updated every 10 seconds. Hardware resource consumption information is shown for: • TCAM entries • Policers • L4 Port Ranges Public ACL and Policy hardware resource commands 212 Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show resources [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip proxy-arp command do?

The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP for the specified Layer 3 interface. Proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces, LAG interfaces, and VLAN interfaces. It is disabled by default. To enable proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables proxy ARP for the specified interface. Syntax: no ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The no form of this command disables BFD on all active OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 o spfv3 bfd. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD echo packets interval. If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Specifies the minimum reception interval in milliseconds. A valu e of 0 means that the switch does not support reception of BFD echo packets. Range: 0, 50 to 1000. Default: 500. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: no bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor ebgp-multihop command do?

The neighbor ebgp-multihop command enables BGP to establish a session with external peers residing on networks that are not directly connected. By default, BGP can only establish sessions with external BGP peers that are directly connected. If external BGP peer are not connected directly, then ebgp-multihop is enabled to initiate the connection with that external peer. If ebgp-multihop is disabled and external BGP peers are indirectly connected, then BGP peer session is not established. The no form of this command disables the peer ebgp-multihop feature. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <ttl> Enables BGP fast peering session deactivation. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ebgp-multihop <ttl>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The vrf <VRF-NAME> command creates a VRF instance named <VRF-NAME> and then enters its context. Use default for <VRF-NAME> to enter the default VRF configure context. Except for the default VRF, the no form of the command deletes the named VRF instance and any IP configuration for interfaces or SVI linked to default VRF. The default VRF cannot be deleted and a warning is given if attempted. To erase the Route-Distinguisher and Route-Targets, enter the default VRF context and delete them manually one by one. Specifies the VRF name. Range: Up to 32 alphanumeric chara cters. The mgmt VRF cannot be used. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax:
- no vrf <VRF-NAME>
- vrf 482.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir command do?

The ip pim-bidir command enables or disables PIM-Bidir in the designated interface. This command works in the interface context, including loopback. The IP address must be configured on the interface to enable BIDIR-PIM. Public BIDIR-PIM commands 484 Enables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Disables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Syntax: ip pim-bidir [enable | disable].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command changes the maximum time, in seconds, before the router transmits the initial PIM hello message on the current interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to default. The default value is 5. Configures the given value as the hello interval. Range: <0-5>. <DELAY-VALUE>. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

document_title: Command Line Interface Reference Guide For 8320, 8325, 8325H, 8325P

document_title: Command Line Interface Reference Guide For 8320, 8325, 8325H, 8325P

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command changes the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current subnet. The router uses hello packets to inform neighbor routers of its presence and to comput the hello hold time. Hello hold time informs neighbor routers estimated wait time for the next hello packet from the router. Shortening the hello interval reduces the hello hold time. Default is set to 30 seconds. The no form of this command resets the frequency of PIM hello messages back to the default value on the current subnet. Public ip pim-bidir hello-interval 487 Configures the given value as hello interval. Range: 1-300 seco nds. • The default value (30 seconds) covers most deployment needs; change only for explicit convergence or timing requirements. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value>.

document_title: Command Line Interface Reference Guide For 8320, 8325, 8325H, 8325P

document_title: Command Line Interface Reference Guide For 8320, 8325, 8325H, 8325P

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value>.

Public ip pim-bidir override-interval 492 <INTERVAL-VALUE> Specifies override interval value. Range: 500-6000. Default: 2500. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. In chatty networks where there is a chance of Hello packets being lost, configuring a higher hold time reduces the likelihood of adjacency loss. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay command enables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. With lan-prune-delay enabled, the router informs downstream neighbors on the wait time before pruning a flow after receiving a prune request. The no form of this command disables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir override-interval <interval-value> command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir override-interval <interval-value> command configures the override interval that gets inserted into the Override Interval field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The default is set to 2500 milliseconds. The no form of this command resets the override interval to the default value of 2500 milliseconds. Public IPv6 pim6-bidir override-interval 508 Configures the given value as override interval. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir override-interval <interval-value>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command do?

The apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command applies a policy to the current physical interface port or LAG or VLAN context. Only one direction of a policy can be applied to an interface or VLAN at a time, thus using the apply command on an interface or VLAN with an already-applied policy of the same direction will replace the currently applied policy. NOTE The VLAN context supports the in and out directions, which apply to both bridged and routed traffic. The Interface VLAN context only supports the routed-in direction which applies only to routed traffic. The no form of this command removes a policy from the interface or VLANspecified by the current context. Specifies the policy to apply. <POLICY-NAME> in out Selects the inbound (ingress) traffic direction. Selects the outbound (egress) traffic direction. Syntax: no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class mac command do?

The class mac command creates or modifies a MAC traffic class to match specified packets. Class is composed of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. With this command, each class can classify traffic based on MAC header information. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a MAC traffic class (use no with the class command) or an individual MAC traffic class entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies create or modify a MAC class. Specifies the name of this class. Specifies a sequence number for the class entry. Optional. Rang e: 1-4294967295. {match|ignore} Creates a rule to match or ignore specified packets. comment Stores the remaining entered text as a class comment. Syntax: class mac <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. Public ip dns domain-name 977 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command do?

Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax:
- ip dns host 978
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Public erps ring 998 Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <PORT0> <PORT1> <ifname> This command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> In ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Creates or enters the HPE Aruba Networking Central configuration context (config-hpe-anw-central). When a switch is able to connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the HPE Aruba Networking Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the HPE Aruba Networking Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to HPE Aruba Networking Central and will get disconnected again. Syntax: redundancy switchover.

The diag-dump rest basic command display REST paths and diagnostics information for the REST feature. The output of this command includes diagnostics information for HPE Aruba Networking Central. Disables connection to HPE Aruba Networking Central server. When the connection is disabled, the switch does not attempt to connect to the HPE Aruba Networking Central server or fetch central location from any of the three sources (CLI/HPE Green Lake/DHCP). It also disconnects any active connection to the HPE Aruba Networking Central server. Public disable 1276 Enables connection to HPE Aruba Networking Central server. Syntax: diag-dump rest basic.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command do?

The https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command this command enables or disables the Firmware Site Distribution server listening on port 9443. The firmware site distribution allows you to use a switch to distribute a firmware image file to other switches in the same network. This prevents the switches from connecting to the cloud or an external network to download a firmware image file. On enabling the firmware site distribution, it exposes a REST endpoint that allows the switches to download a switch primary or secondary firmware image. 1 NOTE As per the limitation, up to two switches can download the firmware image simultaneously. This endpoint is to be used along with REST /firmware endpoint to handle the firmware download and installation process. The no form of this command disables the firmware site distribution server. Syntax: no https-server rest firmware-site-distribution.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip icmp unreachable command do?

The ip icmp unreachable command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages on the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The unreachable host address originates from the failed packed. Default setting. The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. This command does not prevent other hosts from sending an ICMP unreachable message. Syntax:
- no ip icmp unreachable
- ip icmp unreachable 1315.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command do?

The ip igmp snooping all-vlans command enable IGMP snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables IGMP snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command is issued. When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, IGMP snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global IGMP snooping, allows IGMP snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level IGMP snooping settings. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of IGMP reports over the fabric. By default, when IGMP snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging IGMP reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp snooping command do?

The show ip igmp snooping command shows IGMP snooping configuration information and status for all VLANs. Specify a VLAN ID or a VLAN and a group to display details for only that VLAN or VLAN group. Shows IGMP query packets transmitted (Tx), received (Rx), and error packet counters. Shows IGMP Snooping details for all VLANs, including joined po rts or VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) for each group in the V LAN. Shows IGMP snooping groups information. Include the optional vlan <vlan-id> parameter to display information for groups o n a specific VLAN. Negates any configured parameter. packet-exceptions Troubleshoot issues in L2 multicast bridge entries for data pac kets forwarded to the CPU. static-groups statistics vlan <vlan-id> group Shows MLD snooping static group details, including the number of static groups joined. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> command do?

The ip address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> command sets an IPv4 address for the current layer 3 interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.1 00. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 128. Specifies a secondary IP address. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. This value should be less than or equal to the overall MTU for the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Specifies the IP MTU in bytes. Range: 68 to 9198. Default: 150 0. <VALUE>. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip tcp mss <VALUE> command do?

The ip tcp mss <VALUE> command sets the TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) MSS (Maximum Segment Size) for an interface. When configured, the MSS option in the TCP header is set in the TCP handshake for negotiation during connection establishment. Once the TCP session is established, changing the TCP MSS value on the interface will not affect the existing session. The no form of this command removes the TCP MSS configuration from the interface. NOTE TCP MSS is supported on ROP, SVI, subinterface, and L3 GRE tunnel interface. Specifies the IPv4 TCP MSS in bytes. Range: 68 to 65495. <VALUE> Public ip tcp mss 1415. Syntax: no ip tcp mss <VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip neighbor-flood command do?

The ip neighbor-flood command enables VLAN flooding for the specified VLAN interface when a neighbor link goes down. The no form of this command disables VLAN flooding for the specified VLAN interface. Public IP Neighbor Flood commands 1510 Displays the status of IP Neighbor Flood on the specified interface along with other interface-related attributes. Specifies the interface name (for example, vlan30). Optional. <IFNAME> Displays the interfaces on which IP Neighbor Flood is enabled. Displays the current running configuration. Syntax: ip neighbor-flood.

Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading ze ros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192. 168.5.100. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK>. Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command do?

The ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command enables the IPv6-only-preferred mode on the VLAN interface, and is also supported on the OOBM interface. Once enabled, the DHCP client prioritizes a IPv6 address over an IPv4 address. To obtain a DHCPv4 address, the IPv6-only-preferred command must be disabled, and the DHCPv4 client must be restarted. This command is disabled by default. NOTE Ensure the server supports the IPv6-only-preferred mode option, as the command will not work unless supported by the server. The no form of this command disables IPv6-only-preferred mode. NOTE The IPv6-only-preferred mode command must be configured prior to the ip dhcp command, otherwise it will not take effect. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. hop-limit <HOPLIMIT> Specifies the hop limit. Range: 0-255. Default: 64. Configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. Specifies the MTU size. Range: 1280-65535 bytes. Default: 1 500 bytes. <MTU-VALUE> Configures the ND time in milliseconds between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination. Increase the ns-interval time if the network is slow or if there are persistent retry failures. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives and update it to the default value. Specifies the lifetime of router advertisements sent from this in terface. Range: 4 to 9000 seconds. Syntax: no ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command do?

The aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command enables/disables privilege elevation for the users in an administrators group. When enabled, an administrative user is provided lower privilege access upon successful switch login. The user must execute an enable command and re-authenticate to elevate their privilege to administrator (the administrator level access with ‘#’ prompt). Executing the disable command revokes an administrator privilege and returns the user to the lower privilege access level (the operator level access ‘>’ prompt). The no form of this command disables privilege elevation for the administrator user group. Syntax: no aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear mdns-sd statistics command do?

The clear mdns-sd statistics command clears all mDNS gateway statistics. Adds a filter rule to the service profile. The sequence number configured determines the priority with which the rule is matched. Lower the sequence number, higher is the priority. Following are the filter match parameters: • Service-name: mDNS packets are matched against the service IDs configured under the service name. • Service-instance-name: mDNS packets are matched against the service instance name present in the mDNS packets. When no match criteria is specified in the rule, then the rule can be matched against any mDNS packet. Once the match is found then either the packet can be permitted or denied based on the action specified in the rule. The no form of this command deletes the filter configured in the service profile. Syntax: clear mdns-sd statistics.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command configures the drop unknown mode on the switch to the default vlan-shared. Required: Enable shared VLAN filter on the switch. vlan-exclusive Required: Enable exclusive drop unknown filter per VLAN. This command delays the refresh for some IGMP or MLD protocol-related values. When this command is enabled, IGMP/MLD last_reporter value, source, group, or querier uptime, and the create time and expiry time values will be updated based on the configured timer values. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.

Public IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list 1932 The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command enable MLD snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables MLD snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command is issued. When MLD snooping is enabled globally, MLD snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global MLD snooping, allows MLD snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level MLD snooping settings. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of MLD reports over the fabric. By default, when MLD snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging MLD reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include command do?

The ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include command enables the router to forward multicast data received on the VXLAN L3VNI fabric to this interface, regardless of whether a multicast join was received on this interface or not. This allows the interface to be in the same multicast data path state on both the VSX peers. This command must be enabled on the VSX VXLAN routers to support multicast receivers over ROP or P2P SVI extensions. This command can be enabled on a transit L3 peering between VSX peers. This command is optional when the uplink is an MCLAGsVI. The no form of the command disables forwarding of multicast data on the interface. NOTE • An IP address must be configured on the interface and pim-sparse must be enabled. • This command must be enabled only on one interface per VRF. Syntax: no ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol Specifies the area ID is one of the following formats. • Area ID in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255. • Area ID as a decimal value. Range: 0-4294967295. Specifies the OSPFv3 process ID. Range: 1 to 65535. <AREA-ID> <PROCESS-ID> Configures IPSec AH authentication. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay command do?

The IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay command configures the propagation delay that gets inserted into the LAN prune delay field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 500 ms. Specifies the propagation delay value in ms. Default: 500 ms. R ange: 250-2000. <DELAY-VALUE> Configures the multicast datagram time-to-live (router hop-count) threshold for the interface. Any IP multicast datagrams or state-refresh packets with a TTL less than this threshold will not be forwarded out the interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets to the default of 3 attempts. Public IPv6 pim6-dense ttl-threshold 2776 Specifies the time-to-live threshold. Default: 3 attempts. Ran ge: 0-255. Syntax: IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 pim6 rpf (PIM-DM) command do?

The show IPv6 pim6 rpf (PIM-DM) command displays PIM RPF details for the specified source or RP address in the given VRF and shows the nexthop and interface through which the shortest path to the source is available. It also displays if a PIM neighbor is present on the nexthop. Optionally, you can specify display information by VRF. If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. <IP-ADDRESS> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> vsx-peer Optional. Specifies an IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxx x: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. Shows PIM RPF details for the given IP address. Optional. Shows PIM interface information for all VRFs. Optional. Shows PIM interface information for a particular VRF. I f the <VRF-NAME> is not specified, it shows the default VRF i nformation. Syntax: show IPv6 pim6 rpf [<IP-ADDRESS>][all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command do?

The ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command enables the router to accept multicast traffic from remote sources. When enabled, the router accepts multicast traffic from sources that are not directly connected and acts as the First Hop Router (FHR), performing registration to the Rendezvous Point (RP). In scenarios where a Gateway BD is configured in EVPN OISM networks, this should be applied to the external router interface that connects to the Gateway BD. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. Syntax:
- no ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source
- ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source (IPv4) 2832.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip pim-sparse bfd command do?

The ip pim-sparse bfd command configures BFD on a per-interface basis for one interface associated with the PIM process. The no form of this command removes the BFD configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. NOTE If BFD is enabled globally, it will be enabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is disabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd disable command. If BFD is disabled globally, it will be disabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is enabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd command. Disables the BFD configuration on the interface. Syntax: ip pim-sparse bfd [disable].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: no crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. Public crypto pki certificate 3081 The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth.

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period command configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Configures the period the port waits to retransmit the next EAPOL request identity frame on an 802.1X enabled port that has no authenticated clients. The no form of the command resets the discovery period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the discovery period (in seconds). Default: 30. Range: 1 to 65535. <PERIOD> Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator discovery-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. <NAME> Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). Public qos queue-profile 3334. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication allow-fail-through command do?

The aaa authentication allow-fail-through command enables authentication fail-through. If this feature is enabled, the next server or authentication method is tried after an authentication failure. The no form of this command disables authentication fail-through. The system only attempts to reach the next server or authentication method if there is an accounting failure due to an unreachable TACACS+/ RADIUS server or a shared key mismatch error between the switch and the server. NOTE If your switch uses command authorization, best practices is to configure authorization fail-through before configuring authentication fail-through. If not, the switch may fall into an unusable state where authorization will fail for all commands. Syntax:
- no aaa authentication allow-fail-through
- aaa authentication allow-fail-through 3428.

syntax:
- no aaa authentication allow-fail-through
- aaa authentication allow-fail-through 3428

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication login (remote) command do?

The aaa authentication login (remote) command defines authentication as being local (with the name local) (the default). Or defines a sequence of remote AAA server groups to be accessed for authentication purposes. Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually as either local or using remote AAA server groups. All server groups named in your command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. Local is always available for any connection type not configured for remote AAA authentication. NOTE If you do not want local authentication to occur in cases where all AAA servers contacted reject the user's credentials, do not enable authentication fail-through (command aaa authentication allow-fail-through). Syntax: aaa authentication login <CONNECTION-TYPE> {local | group <GROUP-LIST>}.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command do?

The radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before trying the next RADIUS server. If a value is not specified, a default value of 5 seconds is used. You can override this value with a fine-grained per server timeout configured for individual servers. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global authentication timeout to the default of 5 seconds. Specifies the timeout interval of 1 to 60 seconds. Default: 5 sec onds. Syntax: no radius-server timeout [<1-60>].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh ciphers command do?

The ssh ciphers command configures SSH to use a set of ciphers in the specified priority order. Ciphers in SSH are used for privacy of data being transported over the connection. The first cipher type entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Each option is an algorithm that is used to encrypt the link and each name indicates the algorithm and cryptographic parameters that are used. Only ciphers that are entered by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of ciphers and reverts SSH to use the default set of ciphers. Syntax: ssh ciphers <CIPHERS-LIST>.

Note that the admin password is considered set even if it is configured to be empty. The no form of the command disables the SSH server on the specified VRF. If no VRF is specified, by default the SSH server will be enabled on the default or mgmt VRF, depending on the switch model. Public ssh server vrf 3885 Specifies the VRF name. Syntax: no ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME>.

The vrf <VRF-NAME> [name <WORD>] command adds the destination IPv4 static route on the specified VRF. If no <VRF-NAME> is specified the route is applied to the default VRF. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 static route from the VRF. <DEST-IPv6-ADDR> <MASK> <NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> <INTERFACE> nullroute reject Specifies the route destination in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 128. Specifies the next hop in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a deci mal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the next hop as an outgoing interface. Silently discards packets to the destined route. Discards packets to the destined route and returns an ICMP er ror to the sender. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Syntax: vrf <VRF-NAME> [name <WORD>].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show synce status command do?

The show synce status command displays the current status of selected quality level, selected port name, and the SyncE clock state. When the selected clock source is the internal clock, the selected clock quality is the quality of the internal clock and depends on the network type configured. When the SyncE service is not running such as when there is no port with ESMC enabled or missing configuration, then the clock quality is shown as "N/A". Network type to internal clock quality mapping: • Option - QL-SEC • Option - QL-SMC Enables the synchronous ethernet feature on the device. The no form of the command disables the feature. Sets the time in milliseconds for clock source interfaces to be down before beginning new clock selection. Syntax: show synce status.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show user-group command do?

The show user-group command shows user group information for the built-in groups plus any user-defined local user groups. When entered without <GROUP-NAME>, summary information is shown for all groups. Narrows the show command output to that of the specified gro up, and for local user groups, adds the User Group Rules list. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. <GROUP-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the following information for the logged-in user: • User name. • User authentication type: local, RADIUS, or TACACS+. • User group: administrators, operators, or <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: show user-group [<GROUP-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. Public vlan (interface) 4274 If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. <VLAN-LIST> Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

Displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. Syntax: show lacp aggregates [<LAG-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync aaa command do?

The vsx-sync aaa command enables VSX synchronization of all AAA configurations, including user, RADIUS server, and TACACS+ server, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync arp-security command do?

The vsx-sync arp-security command enables VSX synchronization of the ARP security configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. After you enter vsx-sync arp-security, you must enter vsx-sync mclag-int erfaces for enabling VSX synchronization for the ARP security feature. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of ARP security configurations on VLAN mode and LAG interface mode to the secondary peer switch. However, it does not remove the existing ARP security configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync arp-security.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multip lier, min-transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. NOTE This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. Public vsx-sync bfd-global 4451 The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global.

The vsx-sync icmp-tcp command enables VSX synchronization of IP ICMP configurations, including ip icmp unreachable, ip icm p redirect, and ip icmp throttle configurations, on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables the VSX synchronization of IP ICMP configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the existing IP ICMP configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync icmp-tcp.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync keychain command do?

The vsx-sync keychain command enables synchronizing of key chain configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration synchronization from secondary to primary peer. If any additional modification or configuration is made on the primary for the key chain set of features, the features will be auto-synchronized. The no form of the command disables synchronizing key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synchronized configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync keychain.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command do?

The vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command enables the VSX synchronization of VSX LAG interface associations and attributes on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The Usage section in this topic provides a listing of specific associations and attributes that are synchronized to the secondary switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global VSX LAG and attributes, but it does not remove the existing VSX LAG feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync nd-snooping command do?

The vsx-sync nd-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of ND snooping configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize ND snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. When RA guard policy is enabled, this command also synchronizes RA guard policy related configurations. The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Public vsx-sync neighbor 4472. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync qos-global command do?

The vsx-sync qos-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, such as CoS map, DSCP map, and trust policy, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync qos command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, but it does not remove the existing global QoS feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax:
- no vsx-sync qos-global
- vsx-sync qos-global 4476.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE When vsx-sync for BGP or OSPF is configured, route map configurations are synchronized from primary VSX node to the secondary peer due to the dependency in configurations. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip forward-protocol udp command do?

The ip forward-protocol udp command defines the UDP server to which the interface forwards ingress UDP broadcast packets received on a specific UDP port. A maximum of 8 UDP broadcast servers can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes traffic forwarding for the specified server and port/protocol. <IPv4-ADDR> <PORT-NUM> <PROTOCOL> Specifies the UDP server IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), wh ere x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the UDP port number for which traffic is forwarded. Specifies the protocol name for which traffic is forwarded. Syntax: ip forward-protocol udp <IPv4-ADDR> {<PORT-NUM> | <PROTOCOL>}.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show vlan voice command do?

The show vlan voice command displays the voice VLAN list showing the VLAN ID, name, operational state of the VLAN, and the interfaces associated with the VLAN. Public show vlan voice 4267 Disables a VLAN. (By default, a VLAN is automatically enabled when it is created with the vlan command.) The no form of this command enables a VLAN. Configures the VLAN range reserved for internal use for route-only ports and LAGs. The internal VLAN range cannot include any VLANs that are already in use. If the number of internal VLANs is less than the number of route-only ports and LAGs, some ports will be blocked and unable to be used. When the internal VLAN range is modified, traffic on route-only ports and LAGs is briefly interrupted while they are moved to the new range. Syntax: show vlan voice.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan (interface) command do?

The vlan (interface) command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. Public vlan (interface) 4274 If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. <VLAN-LIST>. Syntax: vlan <VLAN-LIST>.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface lag multi-chassis command do?

The interface lag multi-chassis command configures a given LAG as a dynamic multichassis LAG (VSX LAG), which supports a maximum of four member links per switch segment. A VSX LAG across a downstream switch can have at most a total of eight member links. The no form of this command removes a VSX LAG. <LAG-ID> static Specifies the LAG ID. Run the show capacities vsx c ommand for the maximum number of VSX LAGs supported for your particular type of switch; however, the maximum VSX LAG value considers that one port is used for the ISL, which is not a VSX LAG. Required. Specifies the multichassis LAG as static. Optional. Public interface lag multi-chassis 4360. Syntax: interface lag <LAG-ID> multi-chassis [static].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip icmp redirect (VSX) command do?

The ip icmp redirect (VSX) command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Enabled by default. The no form of this command disables ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Sets the dead-interval for keepalive protocol. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for hellos from a peer before declaring the peer to be dead. The default dead-interval is 3 seconds. The no form of this command sets the interswitch link dead-interval to the default of 3 seconds. Public keepalive dead-interval 4363 dead-interval <DEAD- INTERVAL> Specifies the dead-interval in seconds. Range: 2 to 20 second s Sets the hello-interval for keepalive protocol. The hello interval determines the frequency of a hello packet exchange to confirm the peer is alive. Syntax: ip icmp redirect.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command excludes the neighbor configurations from VSX sync operations at run time. When VSX sync is configured for BGP, the VSX secondary peer cannot have any configuration of its own. If the VSX primary has a configuration, that will always get synchronized to the VSX secondary. Any modification done in the synchronized configurations on the VSX secondary will be overwritten by the VSX primary configuration after a VSX config sync. If there is a new configuration on the VSX secondary, then that configuration also gets deleted after a VSX config sync. On the VSX primary, a configuration with vsx-sync-exclude enabled excludes the neighbor configurations from synchronizing to the VSX -secondary, allowing the VSX secondary can have it own entry for the corresponding neighbor configurations. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude.

Initial sync status. • • LAGs on which the delay timer is running. • Status of the LAGs excluded from the link-up delay timer. • • Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split. Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. Syntax: show vsx status [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] [vsx-peer].

When you enter show interface command on the secondary switch, the output from the command indicates that the interface was blocked by VSX feature. Enables split recovery mode. Split recovery mode is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables split-recovery mode. This command configures the forwarding action for packets received on an l2 VLAN port when switch system MAC address as the destination MAC address. The no form of this command configures the switch to the default setting of dropping packets. Syntax: show vsx status shutdown-on-split.

The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. {[active-gateways] [policies]} access-gateways policies. messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Overrides the automatic assignment of the multicast group IP for the VNI flood replication in underlay multicast replication mode. This command is only usable if underlay-multicast replication mode is configured. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv6 format (X: X: : X: X). Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].
